page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by a dedicated 2.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It features 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs, providing a one-to-one ratio of compute cores to memory with 16.0 GB of RAM. The CPU includes a 33 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache. It supports hardware extensions such as AVX, AVX512F, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the instance in the average performance tier. Single-core CPU performance is measured at 1851.77 in stress-ng and 1435.0 in Geekbench, while multi-core performance reaches 15448.63 in stress-ng and 8756.0 in Geekbench. Memory bandwidth tests show 842.59 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 99.23 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 57.5 GB/sec for large-block writes. In application-specific benchmarks, the server handles 15.61 million Redis SET operations per second and achieves a static web serving throughput of 16.35 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning, prompt processing with a 135M parameter LLM reaches 1253.79 tokens per second, while a 7B model processes prompts at 31.77 tokens per second.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and low memory-to-vCPU ratio, the ecs.u1-c1m1.4xlarge offers a compute-dense profile. The lack of bundled local storage and modest 16.0 GB RAM capacity relative to its 16 vCPUs represent clear tradeoffs, making it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data caching. However, it provides a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded CPU tasks, software compilation, text processing, web serving, and lightweight model inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-focused server instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 15448.63 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8756.0. The server demonstrates solid utility for compute-bound tasks, achieving 15.61 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 16.35 GB/sec in static web serving. With a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it represents a highly specialized hardware profile that trades memory capacity for raw compute density. It is best suited for parallel CPU processing, software compilation, web serving, and lightweight text or image processing workloads.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.4xlarge is a compute-focused instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for parallel CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m1.4xlarge features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM, delivering balanced compute performance for parallel workloads.
tagline: A dedicated compute-dense instance featuring a one-to-one ratio of vCPUs to memory for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM
No local storage and no GPU accelerators included
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on a KVM hypervisor
15.61 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and parallel CPU tasks
